role.title: Senior Principal Engineer and Enterprise AI Systems Architect
role.experience: 15+ years building mission-critical, real-time AI systems
role.expertise: Voice AI, conversational systems, enterprise SaaS platforms
role.benchmark: Intimately familiar with Micro1 and Mercor AI interview platform standards

audit.objective: Conduct an exhaustive, brutally honest enterprise-readiness audit of this AI interview system. Score each section as PASS, PARTIAL, or FAIL. Do not give the benefit of the doubt — if something could break in production under load, it is a FAIL.

competitor.name: Micro1
competitor.strength: AI-powered technical assessments with real-time code evaluation
Automated skill verification with anti-cheat detection
Sub-2-second response latency in voice interviews
Structured scoring rubrics with statistical normalization
Candidate experience with minimal friction (single-link access)
Deterministic question sequencing with adaptive difficulty

competitor.name: Mercor
competitor.strength: End-to-end hiring pipeline (screen, interview, match, hire)
AI interviewer with natural conversational flow
Real-time candidate ranking with confidence intervals

section.name: Voice and Real-Time Reliability
section.description: Evaluate the complete voice interview pipeline from browser to AI model and back.

checks.check: End-to-end latency: First byte of AI response under 2 seconds after candidate stops speaking
WebSocket connection stability: Auto-reconnect with state preservation
Audio quality: Echo cancellation, noise suppression, gain control
Graceful degradation: What happens when voice fails? Fallback to text?
Concurrent session capacity: Can it handle 100+ simultaneous interviews?
Browser compatibility: Chrome, Safari, Firefox, Edge — all tested?
Mobile support: Does voice work on iOS Safari and Android Chrome?
Network resilience: Works on 3G/slow connections? Handles packet loss?
Recording integrity: Is the full conversation recorded and verifiable?
Reconnection: If candidate disconnects for 30s, can they resume seamlessly?

section.bar: Zero dropped interviews. Candidate never has to restart. AI never talks over the candidate.

section.name: Conversational Intelligence
section.description: Evaluate how natural, intelligent, and adaptive the AI interviewer is.

checks.check: Does the AI ask relevant follow-up questions based on candidate answers?
Does the AI avoid repeating questions already answered?
Does the AI remember context from earlier in the conversation?
Can the AI handle unexpected answers gracefully (tangents, clarifications)?
Does the AI maintain a consistent persona throughout?
Does the AI adapt difficulty based on candidate performance?
Can the AI handle multi-turn technical discussions?
Does the AI avoid hallucinating — never references things the candidate did not say?
Does the AI properly probe vague answers instead of accepting them?
Is the conversation natural or does it feel scripted/robotic?
Does the AI handle silence appropriately (wait vs prompt)?
Can the AI conduct interviews in multiple languages?

section.bar: Indistinguishable from a skilled human interviewer in conversational flow. Candidates should not feel like they are talking to a bot.

section.name: Assessment and Scoring Integrity
section.description: Evaluate how interviews are scored, validated, and reported.

checks.check: Is scoring rubric-based with clear criteria, not vibes?
Are scores reproducible? Same answers produce same score (plus or minus 5%)?
Is evidence cited for every score? (transcript references)
Are there anti-gaming measures? (detecting rehearsed/GPT answers)
Is there bias detection? (scoring variance across demographics)
Are confidence intervals provided with scores?
Is the scoring model versioned? Can you trace which model scored what?
Are edge cases handled? (candidate who only answers yes/no)
Is there human review capability for borderline cases?
Are scores normalized across different interview sessions?
Is there integrity verification? (proctoring, tab-switch detection)
Can the system detect if someone else is feeding answers?

section.bar: Legally defensible scoring. Explainable AI decisions. No black-box assessments.

section.name: Candidate Experience

checks.check: Single-link access — no account creation required?
Works on any device without app installation?
Clear instructions before interview starts?
Estimated duration shown upfront?
Graceful error messages (not technical jargon)?
Can candidate pause and resume?
Accessibility: Screen reader support? Keyboard navigation?
Accommodations: Extra time, captions, text-only mode?
Post-interview: Does candidate get feedback or just silence?
Data privacy: Clear consent flow? GDPR-compliant data handling?
Load time: Interview page loads in under 3 seconds?
Professional look and feel? Branded for the hiring company?

section.bar: NPS greater than 70 from candidates. Zero candidates abandoned due to technical issues.

section.name: Recruiter and Hiring Manager Experience
section.description: Evaluate the tools available to the hiring side.

checks.check: Dashboard with at-a-glance interview status and results?
Ability to customize interview templates per role?
Bulk invite candidates (CSV upload, ATS integration)?
Real-time notifications when interviews complete?
Shareable report links with access controls?
Comparative candidate ranking within a role?
Filtering and search across completed interviews?
Export capabilities (PDF reports, CSV data)?
Interview reliability/confidence indicators visible?
Audit trail: Who viewed what report, when?
Team collaboration: Multiple reviewers per candidate?
ATS integration: Greenhouse, Lever, Workday, Ashby?

section.bar: Recruiter makes a hiring decision in under 5 minutes after reading the report. Zero back-and-forth needed.

section.name: Security and Compliance

checks.check: SOC 2 Type II compliance (or path to it)?
GDPR: Right to erasure, data portability, consent management?
SSO: SAML/OIDC integration for enterprise clients?
Role-based access control (RBAC)?
Data encryption at rest and in transit?
PII handling: Is candidate data properly isolated?
Data retention policies: Auto-delete after configurable period?
Penetration testing: Recent results available?
API authentication: JWT/OAuth2 with proper token rotation?
Rate limiting on all public endpoints?
Audit logging: Every action traced to a user?
Secrets management: No hardcoded keys, proper env var handling?
OWASP Top 10: SQL injection, XSS, CSRF all covered?
Recording storage: Encrypted, access-controlled, retention-managed?

section.bar: Enterprise procurement team approves without security questionnaire escalation.

section.name: Scalability and Infrastructure

checks.check: Database: Connection pooling, query optimization, index coverage?
Caching: Redis/equivalent for session state and hot data?
CDN: Static assets served from edge?
Auto-scaling: Handles traffic spikes without manual intervention?
Monitoring: Error tracking, latency dashboards, alerting?
Uptime SLA: 99.9%+ achievable with current architecture?
Disaster recovery: Backups, failover, RTO/RPO defined?
Load testing: Validated at 10x current peak?
Cost efficiency: Optimized AI model usage (not burning tokens)?
Multi-region: Can serve candidates globally with low latency?

section.bar: Zero downtime during business hours. P95 API latency under 500ms.

section.name: AI Model and Prompt Engineering

checks.check: Model selection appropriate for task? (not over/under-powered)
Prompt engineering: Structured, versioned, tested?
Guardrails: AI cannot go off-topic, be offensive, or leak system prompts?
Temperature/sampling: Deterministic enough for consistent interviews?
Token budget management: Interviews do not hit context limits?
Model fallback: If primary model is down, what happens?
Evaluation: Are AI outputs systematically evaluated for quality?
Prompt injection protection: Candidate cannot jailbreak the interviewer?
Hallucination rate: Measured and tracked over time?
Response quality: Grammatically correct, professional tone?

section.bar: AI interviewer passes the "would a VP of Engineering trust this?" test.

per-section.field: Specific file paths and line numbers with issue descriptions

final-verdict.field: Recommended prioritized fix list with specific file paths

rules.rule: Read every file in the codebase that is relevant. Do not guess.
Test actual behavior, not just code presence. Does it work or just exist?
Be specific — cite file paths and line numbers for every finding.
Compare against what Micro1 and Mercor actually deliver, not aspirational features.
If you cannot verify something, mark it as UNVERIFIED, not PASS.
Check all validation gates, error handling paths, and edge cases in the code.
Verify that feature flags are correctly wired and defaults are production-safe.
Examine test coverage — untested enterprise features are a FAIL.
Review the database schema for missing indexes, orphaned relations, and data integrity gaps.
Assess whether reconnection, memory, and state management actually work end-to-end, not just in isolation.
